content:
# Luke Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), Folios 42r-43v (PDF pages 83-85) | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/LUK_001_page_001.jpg, LUK_001_page_002.jpg, LUK_001_page_003.jpg

---

**1** Since-indeed many took-in-hand to draw-up a narrative concerning the things having-been-fully-accomplished among us,

**2** just-as delivered [them] to us the-ones from [the] beginning eyewitnesses and servants having-become of-the word,

**3** it-seemed-good also-to-me, having-followed-closely all-things from-above accurately, in-order to-write to-you, most-excellent Theophilos,

**4** so-that you-might-know concerning the words about-which you-were-instructed the certainty.

**5** There-was in the days of-Herod the king of-Judea a-certain priest by-name Zacharias, from [the] daily-course of-Abijah, and a-wife to-him from the daughters of-Aaron, and the name of-her Elisabet.

**6** And they-were both righteous before God, going in all the commandments and ordinances of-the Lord blameless.

**7** And there-was not a-child to-them, because Elisabet was barren, and both were advanced in the days of-them.

**8** And it-came-to-pass in the serving-as-priest of-him in the order of-the daily-course of-him before God,

**9** according-to the custom of-the priesthood, it-fell-to-him-by-lot to-burn-incense, having-entered into the temple of-the Lord;

**10** and all the multitude of-the people was praying outside at-the hour of-the incense.

**11** And there-appeared to-him an-angel of-[the]-Lord, having-stood at-the-right-side of-the altar of-the incense.

**12** And Zacharias was-troubled having-seen [him], and fear fell upon him.

**13** But the angel said to him: Do-not fear, Zacharias, because your petition was-heard, and your wife Elisabet will-bear a-son to-you, and you-shall-call the name of-him Ioannes.

**14** And there-will-be joy to-you and gladness, and many upon the birth of-him will-rejoice.

**15** For he-will-be great before the Lord, and wine and strong-drink he-shall by-no-means drink, and [with] [the]-Holy Spirit he-will-be-filled even from [the] womb of-his mother,

**16** and many of-the sons of-Israel he-will-turn to [the]-Lord the God of-them.

**17** And he will-go-before before him in [the]-spirit and power of-Elijah, to-turn hearts of-fathers upon children and disobedient-ones to [the]-wisdom of-righteous-ones, to-make-ready for-[the]-Lord a-people having-been-prepared.

**18** And Zacharias said to the angel: By what will-I-know this? For I am an-old-man, and my wife [is]-advanced in the days of-her.

**19** And the angel answering said to-him: I am Gabriel, the-one having-stood-before before God, and I-was-sent to-speak to you and to-announce-good-news to-you these-things;

**20** and behold, you-will-be being-silent and not being-able to-speak until the day-in-which these-things come-to-pass, because you-did-not-believe my words, which will-be-fulfilled in the season of-them.

**21** And the people was waiting-for Zacharias, and they-were-marvelling at the delaying of-him in the temple.

**22** And having-come-out he-was not able to-speak to-them, and they-knew that a-vision he-has-seen in the temple; and he was making-signs to-them, and was-remaining mute.

**23** And it-came-to-pass, when the days of-the service of-him were-fulfilled, he-went-away to the house of-him.

**24** And after these days Elisabet the wife of-him conceived, and she-was-hiding herself months five, saying:

**25** Thus the Lord has-done to-me in [the]-days in-which he-looked-upon [me] to-take-away my reproach among men.

**26** And in the month the sixth the angel Gabriel was-sent from God to a-city of-Galilee to-which [the]-name [was] Nazareth,

**27** to a-virgin having-been-betrothed to-a-man to-whom [the]-name [was] Ioseph, from [the]-house of-David, and the name of-the virgin [was] Mariam.

**28** And having-entered to her he-said: Hail, having-been-graced-one, the Lord [is] with you.

**29** And she was-greatly-troubled at the word, and was-pondering what-sort-of greeting this might-be.

**30** And the angel said to-her: Do-not fear, Mariam; for you-found grace with God.

**31** And behold, you-will-conceive in [the]-womb and you-will-bear a-son, and you-shall-call the name of-him Iesous.

**32** This-one will-be great and Son of-[the]-Most-High will-be-called, and [the]-Lord God will-give to-him the throne of-David the father of-him,

**33** and he-will-reign over the house of-Iakob into the ages, and of-the kingdom of-him there-will-not-be an-end.

**34** And Mariam said to the angel: How will-this-be, since a-man I-do-not-know?

**35** And the angel answering said to-her: [The]-Holy Spirit will-come upon you, and [the]-power of-[the]-Most-High will-overshadow you; therefore also the-thing-being-born holy will-be-called Son of-God.

**36** And behold, Elisabet the kinswoman of-you, she-also has-conceived a-son in old-age of-her, and this is [the]-month sixth to-her, the-one being-called barren;

**37** because not-will-be-impossible with God every word.

**38** And Mariam said: Behold, the slave-woman of-[the]-Lord; may-it-come-to-pass to-me according-to the word of-you. And the angel departed from her.

**39** And having-arisen Mariam in the days these went with haste into the hill-country, into a-city of-Judah,

**40** and she-entered into the house of-Zacharias, and greeted Elisabet.

**41** And it-came-to-pass, as Elisabet heard the greeting of-Mariam, the infant leaped in the womb of-her; and Elisabet was-filled [with the]-Holy Spirit,

**42** and she-cried-out [with]-a-cry great and said: Blessed [are]-you among women, and blessed [is] the fruit of-the womb of-you.

**43** And from-where [is]-this to-me, that the mother of-the Lord of-me should-come to me?

**44** For behold, as the voice of-the greeting of-you came into the ears of-me, the infant leaped in gladness in the womb of-me.

**45** And blessed [is] the-one having-believed that there-will-be a-fulfillment to-the-things having-been-spoken to-her from [the]-Lord.

**46** And Mariam said: My soul magnifies the Lord,

**47** and my spirit rejoiced upon God the Savior of-me,

**48** because he-looked-upon the lowliness of-the slave-woman of-him. For behold, from the now all the generations will-call-blessed me,

**49** because the Mighty-One did to-me great-things, and holy [is] the name of-him,

**50** and the mercy of-him [is] into generations and generations to-the-ones fearing him.

**51** He-did might with [the]-arm of-him; he-scattered proud-ones [in the]-thought of-[the]-heart of-them;

**52** he-brought-down powerful-ones from thrones and exalted lowly-ones;

**53** hungry-ones he-filled-with good-things and rich-ones he-sent-away empty.

**54** He-took-hold-of Israel [the]-servant of-him, to-remember mercy,

**55** just-as he-spoke to the fathers of-us, to-Abraham and to-the seed of-him into the age.

**56** And Mariam remained with her about months three, and returned to the house of-her.

**57** And to-Elisabet was-fulfilled the time of-the bearing of-her, and she-bore a-son.

**58** And the neighbors of-her and the kinsmen of-her heard that [the]-Lord was-magnifying the mercy of-him with her, and they-were-rejoicing-with her.

**59** And it-came-to-pass on the day the eighth, they-came to-circumcise the child, and they-were-calling it by-the name of-the father of-him, Zacharias.

**60** And the mother of-him answering said: No, but he-will-be-called Ioannes.

**61** And they-said to her: There-is no-one from the kinship of-you who is-called by-this name.

**62** And they-were-making-signs to-the father of-him, [as-to]-what he-might-wish to-be-called him.

**63** And having-asked-for a-writing-tablet, he-wrote saying: Ioannes is the name of-him. And they-all marvelled.

**64** And was-opened the mouth of-him immediately, and the tongue of-him, and he-was-speaking blessing God.

**65** And fear came upon all the-ones dwelling-around them, and in the whole hill-country of-Judea all these words were-being-talked-about,

**66** and all the-ones having-heard placed [them] in the heart of-them, saying: What then will this child be? For indeed [the]-hand of-[the]-Lord was with him.

**67** And Zacharias the father of-him was-filled [with the]-Holy Spirit, and he-prophesied saying:

**68** Blessed [be the]-Lord the God of-Israel, because he-visited and made redemption for-the people of-him,

**69** and he-raised-up a-horn of-salvation for-us in the house of-David the servant of-him,

**70** just-as he-spoke through [the]-mouth of-the holy from-[the]-age prophets of-him,

**71** salvation from the enemies of-us and from [the]-hand of-all the-ones hating us;

**72** to-do mercy with the fathers of-us, and to-remember [the]-holy covenant of-him,

**73** [the]-oath which he-swore to Abraham the father of-us,

**74** to-give to-us, having-been-rescued from [the]-hand of-the enemies of-us, fearlessly to-serve him,

**75** in holiness and righteousness before him all the days of-us.

**76** And you also, child, a-prophet of-[the]-Most-High you-will-be-called; for you-will-go before [the]-face of-[the]-Lord to-prepare the ways of-him,

**77** to-give knowledge of-salvation to-the people of-him in forgiveness of-sins of-them,

**78** through [the]-tender-mercies of-[the]-God of-us, in which will-visit us a-rising from on-high,

**79** to-shine-upon the-ones in darkness and [the]-shadow of-death sitting, to-direct the feet of-us into [the]-way of-peace.

**80** And the child was-growing and was-being-strengthened in-spirit, and he-was in the wilderness until [the]-day of-[the]-showing-forth of-him to Israel.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### Manuscript Observations (Codex Sinaiticus)

1. **v. 3 — "from above" (anōthen):** Sinaiticus reads ΑΝΩΘΕΝ, which can mean "from above" or "from the beginning/from the first." I have rendered it "from-above" preserving the spatial metaphor of the Greek. Other manuscripts agree.

2. **v. 15 — "even from [the] womb":** Sinaiticus reads ΕΤΙ ΕΚ ΚΟΙΛΙΑΣ, "even/still from [the] womb." The ΕΤΙ is important — it marks the prenatal filling as notable.

3. **v. 17 — "to turn hearts of fathers upon children":** The preposition ΕΠΙ with accusative is rendered literally "upon" rather than the smoother "toward" — the Greek construction echoes Malachi 3:24 (LXX 4:6).

4. **v. 28 — Sinaiticus omits "blessed [are] you among women":** This is a significant variant. The phrase ΕΥΛΟΓΗΜΕΝΗ ΣΥ ΕΝ ΓΥΝΑΙΞΙΝ appears in many later manuscripts at v. 28 but is absent in Sinaiticus at this location. It does appear in v. 42 in the mouth of Elisabet. I have followed the Sinaiticus reading.

5. **v. 29 — "she was greatly troubled" (dietarachthē):** Sinaiticus has the compound form ΔΙΕΤΑΡΑΧΘΗ, indicating deep/thorough disturbance, not mere surprise.

6. **v. 35 — "the thing being born holy":** Sinaiticus reads ΤΟ ΓΕΝΝΩΜΕΝΟΝ ΑΓΙΟΝ — "holy" is predicate, not attributive. The construction is: "the-thing-being-born, holy, will-be-called Son of God." I have preserved this word order.

7. **v. 37 — "not will be impossible with God every word":** The Greek order is ΟΥΚ ΑΔΥΝΑΤΗΣΕΙ ΠΑΡΑ ΤΩ ΘΩ ΠΑΝ ΡΗΜΑ. I have preserved the Greek word order, which places the negative emphatically first. ΡΗΜΑ here carries the double meaning of "word" and "thing" — both senses are active.

8. **v. 46 — Sinaiticus reads "Mariam":** Some manuscripts attribute the Magnificat to "Elisabet" instead, but Sinaiticus clearly reads ΜΑΡΙΑΜ. The Sinaiticus reading is followed.

9. **v. 47 — "rejoiced" (aorist):** The Greek ΗΓΑΛΛΙΑΣΕΝ is aorist indicative, not present. I have preserved this aorist "rejoiced" against the present tense "magnifies" (ΜΕΓΑΛΥΝΕΙ) of v. 46. The shift from present to aorist within the Magnificat is deliberate and must be preserved.

10. **v. 50 — "into generations and generations":** Sinaiticus reads ΕΙΣ ΓΕΝΕΑΣ ΚΑΙ ΓΕΝΕΑΣ rather than the variant ΕΙΣ ΓΕΝΕΑΝ ΚΑΙ ΓΕΝΕΑΝ (singular).

11. **v. 66 — "For indeed the hand of the Lord was with him":** Sinaiticus reads ΚΑΙ ΓΑΡ ΧΕΙΡ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ ΗΝ ΜΕΤ ΑΥΤΟΥ. The ΗΝ (imperfect "was") is the Sinaiticus reading. Some manuscripts read the present ΕΣΤΙΝ ("is").

12. **v. 78 — "a rising from on high" (anatolē ex hypsous):** ΑΝΑΤΟΛΗ can mean "rising" (as of a star/sun) or "branch" (messianic). Both senses may be intended. I have rendered it literally as "rising."

### Nomina Sacra
Throughout the Sinaiticus manuscript, standard nomina sacra abbreviations are used: ΘΣ/ΘΥ for ΘΕΟΣ/ΘΕΟΥ, ΚΣ/ΚΥ for ΚΥΡΙΟΣ/ΚΥΡΙΟΥ, ΠΝΑ for ΠΝΕΥΜΑ, ΙΗΛ for ΙΣΡΑΗΛ, etc. These have been expanded in the translation.

### Orthographic Notes
Sinaiticus occasionally shows itacistic spellings (e.g., ΕΙ for Ι, Η for ΕΙ), which are phonetic variants and do not affect meaning. These are normalized in translation.

---
*Translated and annotated by EMET from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century CE), the earliest complete manuscript of the Gospel of Luke.*
